terms.definition: Abnormally rapid heart rate (>100 bpm at rest)
terms.clinical_relevance: Monitor vitals; assess chest pain, fever, dehydration.

terms.definition: Abnormally slow heart rate (<60 bpm at rest)
terms.clinical_relevance: May be normal in athletes; symptomatic bradycardia needs intervention.
Leading risk for stroke and MI; often asymptomatic.
Assess for shock; check orthostatics and fluid status.
Check SpO2, lung sounds; position upright.
Sleep apnea increases cardiovascular risk; monitor post-op.
Early sign of respiratory distress or metabolic acidosis.
May indicate CNS depression from opioids or head injury.
Report gross or microscopic hematuria to provider.
Key indicator of kidney damage; common in diabetes.
Classic sign of hyperglycemia and diabetes.
Output <400 mL/day signals acute kidney injury.
Medical emergency; assess for obstruction or renal failure.
NSAIDs and H. pylori are common causes.
Monitor liver enzymes; types A–E differ in transmission.
Monitor BUN, creatinine, and fluid balance.
Assess triggers; contact vs atopic patterns differ.
Assess pain, ROM, and morning stiffness.
Nuchal rigidity and photophobia are classic signs.
Assess O2, crackles; incentive spirometry helps.
Time-critical; qSOFA and lactate guide escalation.

terms.definition: Deficiency of red blood cells or hemoglobin
terms.clinical_relevance: Assess fatigue, pallor; iron studies guide treatment.
Bleeding risk; monitor for petechiae and bruising.
Assess for DKA/HHS; verify insulin orders.

terms.definition: Metabolic state with ketones and acidosis
terms.clinical_relevance: DKA emergency: fluids, insulin, K+ monitoring.
Check SpO2; central cyanosis is more urgent.
Assess sclera; liver vs hemolytic causes differ.
Pitting vs non-pitting; assess heart, kidney, liver.
Common in liver cirrhosis; monitor respiratory status.
Pressure injuries can progress to necrosis.
Chest pain may indicate myocardial ischemia.
MI and stroke are infarctions — time-critical.
PE is a leading cause of preventable death.

terms.definition: Formation of a blood clot in a vessel
terms.clinical_relevance: DVT prophylaxis is standard for immobile patients.
Discontinue IV and rotate sites to prevent.
Statins can cause myopathy; report muscle pain.
Fall prevention is critical in older adults.
Assess respiratory impact in severe cases.

terms.definition: Excessive outward curvature of thoracic spine

terms.clinical_relevance: Pregnancy and obesity are common causes.
LLQ pain; high-fiber diet for prevention.
Murphy sign positive; often post-fatty meal pain.
Severe epigastric pain radiating to back.
C. diff colitis requires contact precautions.
Dysuria and frequency are hallmark symptoms.
Postpartum infection; monitor lochia and fever.
Bacterial vs viral spread precautions differ.
Contact lens wear is a risk factor.
Oral hygiene prevents progression to periodontitis.
Pain worse supine, better leaning forward.
IV drug use is a major risk factor.

terms.definition: Study and treatment of kidney disease
terms.clinical_relevance: Nurses track I&O and renal labs.

terms.definition: Study and treatment of heart disease

terms.clinical_relevance: Neuro checks q1-4h in acute settings.
Wound assessment and pressure injury staging.
Catheter care and UTI prevention bundles.
Mobility, fall risk, and weight-bearing status.
Insulin and heparin route; rotate sites.
Aspiration no longer recommended; select site by volume.
NPO prep; post-procedure vitals and bleeding.
Bowel prep education is nursing role.
Minimally invasive; shoulder pain from CO2 common.

terms.definition: Surgical puncture to drain pleural fluid
terms.clinical_relevance: Improve breathing; send fluid for analysis.

terms.definition: Surgical puncture to drain peritoneal fluid
Puncture of amnion to withdraw fluid
terms.clinical_relevance: Genetic testing and lung maturity assessment.
Body image support and lymphedema education.
Single kidney — monitor renal function lifelong.
High output — monitor hydration and electrolytes.
Tube feeding administration and site care.
Assess incision and bowel function return.
Informed consent; monitor biopsy site bleeding.
Bleeding precautions; monitor INR for warfarin.
Correct needle length by body habitus.
STEMI injures myocardium — time is muscle.
Widening on CXR may indicate aortic injury.
Gas exchange surface; surfactant prevents collapse.
Midline position; deviation suggests tension pneumo.
NG tube placement confirmed by X-ray.
Digital rectal exam for prostate and occult blood.
Left atrium receives oxygenated blood from lungs.
Dissection presents with tearing chest pain.
Common arterial access for cardiac cath.
Renal dose adjustments for many drugs.
Temperature route — not for accurate core temp.
Otic drops — warm to body temp before instilling.
Do not touch dropper to eye.
IV, IM, SC routes — sterile technique.
Fracture site proximal to wrist is forearm.
Distal pulse check after cast application.
Superior vena cava returns head/arm blood.
IVC returns lower body blood to heart.
Unilateral weakness — stroke vs Bell palsy.
Chronic vs acute guides treatment urgency.
Acute coronary syndrome — ECG within 10 min.

terms.definition: Spread of cancer to distant sites

terms.clinical_relevance: Aggressive; often affects bone and muscle.
ABCDE criteria for skin lesion assessment.
Colon adenoma may progress to carcinoma.
Cervical dilation measured in cm during labor.
Skin turgor and mucous membranes assess.
Root cause analysis in nursing diagnosis.
Pain is a symptom — subjective data.
Fever is a sign — objective measurement.
Metabolic syndrome — cluster of risk factors.
Congenital heart defects need lifelong follow-up.
Chlorhexidine skin prep before IV insertion.
Bleach for C. diff spores on surfaces.
